rationale.P01: api-edge http_5xx_rate 0.82 vs 0.05, pat-edge-upstream-down surging 8800% from ~zero baseline, never auto-resolved, metric still 0.85 at 03:00. Loud independent SEV1 edge outage. Must-page.
rationale.P02: payments-db conn_pool_used_pct pinned at 100% (rose 41->78->100 by 01:42, still 100 at 02:45), pat-pool-exhausted surging 2600%, never auto-resolves. Root of the payments cascade. Independent SEV1. Must-page.
rationale.P03: Downstream symptom of P02: checkout 5xx via pat-pool-exhausted, onset ~45s after the DB page. Collapse into the one payments-pool incident.
rationale.P04: Downstream symptom of P02: orders p99 latency via pat-pool-exhausted, onset ~80s after the DB page. Duplicate of the payments-pool incident.
rationale.P05: Downstream symptom of P02: fulfillment error_rate, same pat-pool-exhausted root. Part of the one cascade.
rationale.P06: log-archiver disk flapper: 14 fires/hr, oscillates at the 85% threshold and self-clears in 23s (back to 83). Chronic churn, neutral pattern delta 0%.
rationale.P07: Same disk flapper as P06, another oscillation (14/hr, cleared in 19s, back to 83.5).
rationale.P08: ingest-buffer disk-rotate flapper: 11 fires/hr, cleared in 26s, metric back to 82. Same chronic class as P06/P07.
rationale.P09: recommendation cpu 94% tagged SEV1 by a coarse rule, but 9 fires/hr and auto-resolved in 31s (back to 46), neutral pat-gc-pause delta 4%. Severity label is wrong; this is a GC-pause flapper.
rationale.P10: metering-pipeline consumer_lag rising monotonically (150k->240k->290k->340k->395k across the window), never auto-resolves, no deploy to blame, pat-metering-lag delta 240% with negative sentiment. Quiet SEV3 slow-burn regression that never recovers. Must-page (recall trap).
rationale.P11: search cpu 92% tagged SEV1, but 7 fires/hr, auto-resolved in 27s (back to 51), neutral pat-gc-pause. Mislabeled GC flapper.
rationale.P12: notifications queue marginally over threshold during batch drain, cleared in 22s (back to 3100). Routine.
rationale.P13: payments-api queue_depth matches already-open INC-9142 (pat-billing-backlog, payments-api queue backlog, owned by oncall-payments). A human is already engaged; do not re-page.
rationale.P14: search-indexer index_error_rate spiked to 0.34 right after deploy idx-2026.05.20-c, dipped to 0.11 at 02:05 (looked self-healed) but climbed back to 0.29-0.31 and is still elevated at 03:00; never auto-resolved, pat-index-write-fail delta 520% negative. Real deploy regression that did NOT recover. Page.
rationale.P15: frontend 5xx bump during fe-2026.05.20-a rollout, auto-resolved in 44s (back to 0.004). Deploy transient.
rationale.P16: frontend cpu settle during the same fe rollout, auto-resolved in 36s (back to 45). Deploy transient.
rationale.P17: cart cold-cache latency blip, self-resolved in 29s (back to 380).
rationale.P18: image-resize cold-cache latency blip, recovered in 25s (back to 410).
rationale.P19: auth cpu 90.5% tagged SEV1, but 12 fires/hr, auto-resolved in 18s (back to 47), neutral pat-gc-pause. Mislabeled GC flapper.
rationale.P20: billing 5xx fractionally over threshold, 3 fires/hr, cleared in 20s (back to 0.006). Flapper.
rationale.P21: Downstream symptom of P02: orders p99 latency via pat-pool-exhausted (same root, ~13min into the cascade). Duplicate of the payments-pool incident.
rationale.P22: recommendation latency blip near a stale rec-2026.05.19-d deploy, auto-resolved in 34s (back to 420). Transient.
rationale.P23: session-store memory blip during cache eviction sweep, auto-resolved in 28s (back to 84). Routine.
rationale.P24: checkout cpu blip, 5 fires/hr, auto-resolved in 24s (back to 52), neutral pat-gc-pause. Flapper that coincides with the cascade onset but is unrelated.
rationale.P25: search-indexer cpu settle during deploy idx-2026.05.20-c, auto-resolved in 32s (back to 49), neutral pat-rollout-503. Deploy transient (distinct from the index_error_rate regression P14).
rationale.P26: gateway-canary synthetic probe 503: 8 fires/hr, auto-resolved in 17s, pat-canary-probe neutral delta 0%, no real traffic. SEV1 label is on a synthetic probe; not a real outage.
rationale.P27: analytics-batch queue marginally over threshold during batch drain, cleared in 21s (back to 2400). Routine.
rationale.P28: Downstream symptom of P02: cart error_rate via pat-pool-exhausted, onset within the cascade window. Duplicate of the payments-pool incident.
rationale.P29: image-resize cpu blip auto-resolved in 30s (back to 48), neutral pat-gc-pause. Flapper.

notes: Four page-class real incidents among heavy noise. (1) Loud SEV1 edge outage: P01 (api-edge, pat-edge-upstream-down, never resolves) - must-page. (2) Correlated payments-pool cascade collapsing to ONE page: P02 root SEV1 (payments-db pool exhausted, never resolves) - must-page; P03/P04/P05/P21/P28 share pat-pool-exhausted and onset window -> suppress as duplicates. (3) Quiet slow-burn: P10 (metering-pipeline consumer_lag rising monotonically, no deploy, never resolves) - SEV3 recall trap, must-page. (4) Deploy regression that looked self-healed but did not: P14 (search-indexer index_error_rate dipped after idx-2026.05.20-c then climbed back and stayed) - real page, non-must (miss_budget 1 tolerates a conservative model dropping it, but doing so costs F1). All other pages are noise: SEV1-mislabeled GC flappers (P09/P11/P19/P26), disk-rotate flappers (P06/P07/P08), recovered deploy transients (P15/P16/P22/P25), batch-drain/cold-cache/cache-evict sub-minute transients (P12/P17/P18/P23/P24/P27/P29/P20), and a duplicate of already-open INC-9142 (P13). must_page = the two SEV1 roots plus the quiet-but-dangerous slow-burn (P01, P02, P10).
